title: Listening - Part 4: Museum Visits
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about visiting a museum. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I visited the Science Museum because [21]I wanted to show my teenage son that engineering could actually be an exciting career path[/21], not just numbers on a page. He’s a bit of a skeptic. To my surprise, [26]it was the interactive nature of the exhibits that really won him over[/26]. We spent hours building a virtual bridge. It made me realise that hands-on learning is far more effective than just reading plaques on a wall. || Speaker 2: I went to the National Gallery [22]specifically to see a particular painting that had been mentioned in a novel I’d just finished[/22]. I wanted to see if the author’s description was accurate. It was breathtaking. But the highlight for me was [27]the peace and quiet I found in the less popular galleries[/27]. In such a hectic city, having a space where you can just sit in silence and reflect is a rare luxury. || Speaker 3: I’m a history teacher, and [23]I was at the local heritage museum to gather material for a new module on the Industrial Revolution[/23]. I needed some local flavour for my lessons. It was very productive, but [28]I was most impressed by the dedication of the elderly volunteers[/28]. Their passion for preserving the town’s history was truly infectious and reminded me why I went into teaching in the first place. || Speaker 4: I visited a contemporary art museum [24]mainly because I’d read a scathing review in the newspaper and wanted to judge it for myself[/24]. I usually disagree with that critic. In the end, I found the art a bit baffling, but [29]the architectural design of the building itself was spectacular[/29]. The way the light interacted with the glass and steel was more impressive than anything hanging on the walls. || Speaker 5: I went to a natural history museum [25]to escape a sudden downpour while I was on holiday[/25]. It was purely a practical move to stay dry. I didn't expect much. However, [30]I ended up having a fascinating conversation with one of the curators[/30]. She was so knowledgeable about fossil preservation that I stayed for two hours just listening to her stories. It turned a rainy afternoon into the highlight of my trip.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker decided to visit the museum.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to inspire their son about an engineering career.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 went to see a painting mentioned in a book they had read.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 was gathering material for their history lessons.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to see if they agreed with a negative review.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 went inside simply to avoid the rain.

options.B: to follow up on a literary interest
options.D: to meet a friend in a central location
options.F: to influence a family member's future
options.G: to research a specific historical period
options.H: to seek shelter from the weather

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker found most notable about their visit.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 was impressed by the 'interactive nature' of the exhibits.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 valued the 'peace and quiet' and the space for reflection.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 was moved by the 'passion' and 'dedication' of the volunteers.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 found the 'architectural design' more impressive than the art.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 enjoyed the 'fascinating conversation' with the curator.

options.B: the quality of the personal interaction
options.H: the aesthetic appeal of the premises
